title: AI Skills Builder
subtitle: Build Your AI Dream Team with Claude Skills & Gemini Gems

sections.title: Welcome
sections.content: STOP CHATTING. START BUILDING. Most people use AI the wrong way — they open a chat, type a question, and start from scratch every single time. Power users do something different. They build Skills — specialized AI assistants that already know their business, their style, and their workflows. This playbook teaches you exactly how to do that on two of the most powerful platforms: Claude and Gemini.

sections.title: How to Use This Playbook
sections.content: Three-step approach to building your AI team.

items.title: Learn the Concept
items.instruction: Each chapter explains one skill-building technique in plain language. No coding required.

items.title: Copy the Template
items.instruction: Every chapter includes ready-to-use templates. Copy them directly into Claude or Gemini.

items.title: Build Your AI Team
items.instruction: By the end, you'll have 10+ specialized AI assistants working for you around the clock.

sections.title: What's Inside
sections.content: How to build Claude Skills & Gemini Gems from scratch. 10+ tested, ready-to-use skill templates. 5 AI team member roles you can set up today. Advanced techniques: voice cloning, prompt chaining, self-improvement loops. Step-by-step screenshots and real examples.

sections.title: Table of Contents
sections.content: 3 parts. 10+ templates. Your AI dream team.

items.title: The Foundations
chapters: Claude Skills: Your AI Specialist Factory (Pages 6-10)

items.title: Advanced Skills
chapters: Voice Cloning: Make AI Sound Like You (Pages 25-26)

sections.title: Chapter 01: What Are AI Skills?
sections.content: The difference between chatting with AI and building with AI.
sections.callout: Chatting with AI = Hiring a new temp every day. They don't know you, your business, or what you did yesterday. Building AI Skills = Hiring permanent specialists. They know your brand, your style, your data, and your preferences — every single time.

features: Create custom workflows with instructions + knowledge files
Can execute code and run complex tasks
Best for: Deep work, long documents, specialized knowledge
Works on: Claude.ai (Pro, Team, Enterprise) + Claude Code + API
Create custom AI assistants with detailed instructions
Native integration with Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Gmail)
Best for: Google Workspace users, quick-access specialists
Works on: Gemini (free for basic, Advanced for full features)

sections.pro_tip: You don't have to choose one. Many power users build their 'thinking' skills in Claude (for deep analysis and writing) and their 'action' skills in Gemini (for tasks connected to Google Docs, Sheets, and Gmail).

sections.title: Chapter 02: Claude Skills — Your AI Specialist Factory
sections.content: Claude Skills are the most powerful way to create AI specialists. Each skill is a set of instructions (and optionally code + reference files) that teaches Claude exactly how to handle a specific type of work.
real_examples: A brand guidelines enforcer that checks every piece of content against your style guide
A financial report generator that formats data exactly how your team expects
A customer email responder trained on your FAQs and policies
A code reviewer that follows your team's coding standards

levels.name: Simple
levels.description: Just a markdown file (SKILL.md) with instructions. Like giving someone a detailed job description.

levels.name: With Knowledge
levels.description: Add reference files (PDFs, docs, data) that Claude can access. Like giving someone a training manual.

levels.name: With Code
levels.description: Add executable scripts for complex workflows. Like giving someone tools to work with.

sections.note: This playbook focuses on Level 1 and 2. No coding needed.

how_to_build.detail: Toggle it on in your skills list
Claude now has this specialist knowledge

skill_md_anatomy.description: Every SKILL.md file has two required parts.
skill_md_anatomy.frontmatter: The --- section: Name + description. Claude reads this to decide WHEN to use your skill. Keep it clear and specific.
skill_md_anatomy.instructions: The markdown body: The actual guidance. This is your specialist's 'brain.' The more specific, the better.
skill_md_anatomy.example:
---
name: Marketing Manager
description: Creates marketing content following brand guidelines, analyzes campaign performance, and suggests growth strategies.
---

# Marketing Manager Skill

## When to Use This Skill
Use this skill when the user asks about marketing strategy, content creation, campaign planning, or brand messaging.

## Instructions
[Your detailed instructions go here]

easy_alternative.title: The Easy Way: Use Claude Projects
easy_alternative.description: Don't want to create SKILL.md files? Claude Projects give you the same power through a simple interface.

steps.detail: Every chat in this project now uses these instructions

easy_alternative.recommendation: Start with Projects. They're easier. Move to Skills when you want more power or need to share with a team.

sections.title: Chapter 03: Gemini Gems — Your Google-Powered Experts
sections.content: If you live in Google Workspace, Gems are your secret weapon. Gems are custom AI assistants inside Gemini. You give them detailed instructions, and they become persistent specialists you can call on anytime. They connect directly to your Google Drive, Docs, Sheets, and Gmail.
gem_features: Native Google integration — they can read your Drive files in real-time
Shareable — send to colleagues with view/use/edit permissions (like sharing a Google Doc)
Pre-made templates — Google offers starter Gems you can customize
Up to 15,000 tokens of instructions — room for very detailed guidance
Works on free plan (basic) and Advanced plan (full features)

how_to_build.detail: Try a sample task to verify it works
Your Gem is now always available in the sidebar

5_part_formula.title: Writing Great Gem Instructions — The 5-Part Formula

parts.label: ROLE
parts.template: "You are my [specific role] with expertise in [areas]."

parts.label: CONTEXT
parts.template: "About my business: [company, industry, audience, products]. My typical tasks for you: [list common requests]."

parts.label: RULES
parts.template: "Always [do this]. Never [do that]. When unsure, [ask/suggest/default to this]."

parts.label: OUTPUT FORMAT
parts.template: "Respond with [structure: bullets, tables, short paragraphs]. Keep responses under [length]. Start with [summary/recommendation/question]."

parts.label: EXAMPLES
parts.template: "Here's what great output looks like: [provide 1-2 examples of ideal responses]."

gem_power_moves.title: Connect to Google Drive
gem_power_moves.description: Upload your key business documents to a Google Drive folder. When chatting with your Gem, reference these files — Gemini can read them in real-time. Your brand guide updates? Your Gem sees the latest version automatically.

gem_power_moves.title: Share with Your Team
gem_power_moves.description: Click the share icon on any Gem → Set permissions (View, Use, or Edit). Your team gets the same specialist without building their own.

gem_power_moves.title: Chain Gems Together
gem_power_moves.description: Use one Gem's output as input for another. Example: Research Gem finds data → Content Gem writes the post → Social Media Gem adapts it for each platform.

comparison_table.title: Claude Projects vs Gemini Gems: Quick Reference
rows: Free tier available, Pro for Projects
Free tier basic, Advanced for full

sections.title: Skill Template 01: The Marketing Manager
sections.description: Your 24/7 marketing strategist who knows your brand inside and out.
sections.when_to_use: Content creation, campaign planning, social media strategy, brand messaging, marketing analysis

template.ROLE: You are my dedicated Marketing Manager. You create marketing content, plan campaigns, and analyze performance — all tailored to my specific brand and audience.

ABOUT_MY_BUSINESS.target_audience: [Describe ideal customer — age, role, pain points, where they hang out online]
ABOUT_MY_BUSINESS.brand_voice: [e.g., professional but approachable / bold and direct / warm and conversational]

YOUR_RESPONSIBILITIES: Create marketing copy that matches my brand voice exactly
Suggest content ideas based on my target audience
Plan campaigns with timelines, budgets, and KPIs
Write social media posts, email sequences, and ad copy
Analyze marketing metrics when I share data
Keep messaging consistent across all channels
RULES: Always write in my brand voice — never generic corporate speak
Every piece of content must have a clear call-to-action
When suggesting strategies, include estimated budget and timeline
Prioritize organic growth strategies for tight budgets
Ask clarifying questions before making assumptions
OUTPUT_FORMAT: Start with a 1-sentence recommendation or summary
Use bullet points for lists and action items
Present options as: Option A / Option B / My Recommendation
Include word counts for any long-form content
For social posts: Hook line | Body | CTA | Hashtags

steps: Paste the template above into Custom Instructions
Upload: brand guidelines PDF, past campaign results, audience research
Create a file called SKILL.md with the frontmatter + template
Claude will automatically use it when you ask marketing questions
Paste the template into the instructions field
Store brand docs in a Google Drive folder for reference

sections.first_task_prompt: I need 5 social media post ideas for this week. My goal is to increase engagement by 20%. Suggest posts for [your platform], each with a different content angle.

sections.title: Skill Template 02: The Content Writer
sections.description: An AI writer that actually sounds like you — not like a robot.
sections.when_to_use: Blog posts, emails, newsletters, website copy, social media captions, any written content

template.ROLE: You are my Content Writer. You write all types of content in MY voice — not generic AI voice. Every piece should sound like I wrote it myself.

MY_WRITING_VOICE.tone: [e.g., conversational and direct / witty and informal / authoritative but warm]
MY_WRITING_VOICE.i_always: [e.g., use short paragraphs, start with a hook, include real examples]
MY_WRITING_VOICE.i_never: [e.g., use cliches like 'in today's world', write long intros, use passive voice]

CONTENT_RULES: Start with a hook — if the first line is boring, rewrite it
Every paragraph must earn its place — cut filler ruthlessly
Use specific examples, not generic advice
No AI cliches: 'dive into', 'landscape', 'in the realm of'
WHEN_I_REQUEST_CONTENT: First confirm: topic, length, platform, and audience
Provide 3 headline/subject line options before writing
Include SEO keywords naturally if I specify them
For long-form: provide outline first, then full draft
OUTPUT_FORMAT: Always show word count at the end
Format for the target platform (short for social, blog)
Bold key takeaways or important lines
If I say 'shorter' — cut by 30%, not 10%

bonus_template.title: The Voice Profile Builder
bonus_template.description: Before using the Content Writer, run this once to extract your writing DNA.
bonus_template.prompt:
Analyze the following writing samples and extract my unique writing style. Create a detailed 'Voice Profile' covering:

1. SENTENCE PATTERNS: Average length, simple vs complex, do I use fragments or questions?
2. VOCABULARY: Formal vs casual, industry jargon used, words I favor
3. TONE: Serious, playful, authoritative, conversational?
4. PARAGRAPH STYLE: Short and punchy vs detailed?
5. OPENINGS: How do I typically start pieces?
6. TRANSITIONS: How do I connect ideas?
7. QUIRKS: Any distinctive habits or patterns?

Format the output as instructions I can paste directly into an AI skill template.

MY WRITING SAMPLES:
[Paste 3-5 samples here — emails, posts, articles, anything you wrote naturally]
bonus_template.pro_tip: Use emails you've actually sent (not formal reports). Emails show your natural voice better than anything else.

sections.title: Skill Template 03: The Financial Analyst
sections.description: Your numbers person who never sleeps and never makes calculation errors.
sections.when_to_use: Financial data analysis, reporting, forecasting, budgeting, expense review, revenue analysis

template.ROLE: You are my Financial Analyst. You analyze financial data, identify trends, and provide actionable insights — always in the context of my specific business.

MY_BUSINESS_CONTEXT.key_metrics: [e.g., MRR, profit margin, CAC, LTV, average order value, inventory turnover]

YOUR_TASKS: Analyze financial data I upload (spreadsheets, reports, bank statements)
Create executive summaries of financial performance
Flag expenses that seem unusual or growing too fast
Help with forecasting and cash flow projections
ANALYSIS_RULES: Always start with a 3-sentence executive summary
Use tables for any data comparisons
Highlight numbers that need attention in bold
Calculate percentage changes, not just raw numbers
Distinguish between one-time events and recurring trends

bonus_template.title: The Data Upload Template
bonus_template.description: When uploading financial data to your analyst, use this briefing template for better results.
bonus_template.template:
DATA BRIEFING:
I've uploaded [file name]. This is [type of data].

CONTEXT:
- Each row represents: [what each row is]
- Key columns: [list the important ones]
- Time period: [date range covered]
- Currency: [if applicable]

WHAT I NEED:
1. [Your specific question]
2. [Second question]
3. [Third question]

COMPARE TO: [Last quarter / same period last year / budget]

FORMAT: Executive summary first, then detailed breakdown.
bonus_template.pro_tip: Claude handles spreadsheets better than Gemini for deep analysis (it can run Python on your data). Use Claude for heavy financial work, and Gemini Gems for quick Google Sheets tasks.

sections.title: Skill Template 04: The Research Assistant
sections.description: A researcher who delivers structured insights, not Wikipedia summaries.
sections.when_to_use: Competitor research, market analysis, industry trends, technology evaluation, due diligence, preparing for meetings

template.ROLE: You are my Research Assistant. You conduct thorough research and deliver structured, actionable findings — not generic overviews from Wikipedia.

MY_RESEARCH_CONTEXT.typical_research_needs: [e.g., competitor analysis, market trends, technology evaluation, supplier research]
MY_RESEARCH_CONTEXT.how_i_use_research: [e.g., presentations, strategy meetings, investment decisions, reports]

RESEARCH_RULES: Always cite sources with URLs when possible
Distinguish facts from opinions — label clearly
Flag outdated info (anything older than 6 months)
When comparing options, always use tables
Note information gaps — tell me what you couldn't find
If a claim seems important, look for a second source
OUTPUT_STRUCTURE: Bottom Line (2 sentences — what I need to know)
Key Findings (5-7 bullet points, most important first)
template.QUALITY_STANDARD: I'd rather have 5 solid findings than 15 surface-level ones. Always provide 'so what?' — why should I care. If two sources disagree, present both and note the conflict.

bonus_templates.name: Competitor Analysis Request
bonus_templates.prompt:
Research [competitor name]. I need:
1. Their product/service lineup and pricing
2. Their target audience (how it overlaps with mine)
3. Their recent moves (last 6 months — launches, hires)
4. Their strengths I should worry about
5. Their weaknesses I could exploit
6. What customers say about them (reviews, complaints)

Present as: Executive brief I can share with my team.

bonus_templates.name: Market Trend Report
bonus_templates.prompt:
Research the current state of [topic/industry]. Cover:
1. Market size and growth rate
2. Top 3 trends shaping this space right now
3. Key players and their market share
4. Opportunities for a [your business type]
5. Risks or threats to watch
6. Timeline: what's happening in the next 6-12 months

Present as: Strategic briefing with a recommendation section.

bonus_templates.name: Technology Evaluation
bonus_templates.prompt:
Compare [Tool A] vs [Tool B] vs [Tool C] for [my use case]. Evaluate on:
1. Features relevant to my needs: [list specific needs]
2. Pricing (include hidden costs)
3. Ease of setup and learning curve
4. Integration with my tools: [list your tools]
5. What real users say (not just marketing claims)
6. Your recommendation and reasoning

Present as: Comparison table + written recommendation.

sections.title: Skill Template 05: The Customer Support Agent
sections.description: Consistent, empathetic customer responses — trained on YOUR policies.
sections.when_to_use: Responding to customer inquiries, drafting support emails, handling complaints, FAQ management

template.ROLE: You are my Customer Support Agent. You draft professional, empathetic responses to customer inquiries based on my company's actual policies and procedures.

POLICIES.common_issues: [List top 5 customer complaints and standard resolutions]
POLICIES.escalation_triggers: [When to recommend a human — e.g., billing disputes over $X, legal threats]

RESPONSE_RULES: Always acknowledge the customer's feeling first
Provide a clear solution or next step
Offer alternatives if the first solution isn't possible
End with: 'Is there anything else I can help with?'
Never promise anything outside our stated policies
Never argue — if wrong, redirect politely
For angry customers: empathize, apologize, solve
template.TONE: Warm, helpful, professional. Never robotic, never overly casual. Think 'friendly expert.'
template.SIGNATURE: [Your preferred sign-off — e.g., 'Best regards, [Name] — [Company] Support Team']

common_support_scenarios.prompt:
Customer wants a refund for [product/reason].

Draft a response that:
1. Acknowledges their frustration
2. Confirms our refund policy applies
3. Explains the process and timeline
4. Offers an alternative (exchange, credit) if applicable
5. Keeps it under 120 words

Customer is unhappy with [product/service quality].

Draft a response that:
1. Thanks them for the feedback
2. Apologizes for the experience
3. Asks specific questions to understand the issue
4. Offers a concrete resolution
5. Assures them of quality commitment

sections.title: Advanced Skill 01: Voice Cloning — Make AI Sound Like You
sections.content: The most powerful skill you can build is one that sounds exactly like you. Here's how to extract and replicate your unique voice.
sections.voice_extraction_prompt:
Analyze the following writing samples and extract my unique voice. Identify:

1. TONE: How formal/casual? Serious or playful?
2. SENTENCE STRUCTURE: Long and complex or short and punchy?
3. VOCABULARY: Technical jargon or plain language? Favorite words/phrases?
4. PATTERNS: How do I start paragraphs? How do I make transitions?
5. PERSONALITY MARKERS: Humor, directness, warmth, confidence level?
6. THINGS I AVOID: What's noticeably absent from my writing?

Then create a 'Voice Replication Guide' I can paste into any AI prompt to make it write like me.

SAMPLES:
[Paste 5+ varied writing samples — emails, posts, reports]
sections.voice_testing_prompt:
Write a [type of content] about [topic] using my Voice Profile below. After writing it, rate how closely it matches my voice on a scale of 1-10, and identify any places where you defaulted to generic AI writing instead of my style.

VOICE PROFILE:
[Paste your extracted voice profile]
sections.pro_tip: The more varied your samples, the better the profile. Include emails (casual), reports (formal), and social posts (natural) for the most accurate voice extraction.

sections.title: Advanced Skill 02: Prompt Chaining — Multi-Step Workflows
sections.content: Single prompts are powerful. Chains are transformative. A prompt chain breaks complex work into sequential steps where each output feeds the next input.
sections.why_chains_work: 1. Each step can be optimized independently. 2. You can review and edit between steps. 3. The AI focuses on one thing at a time — better outputs. 4. Complex tasks become manageable.

steps.label: RESEARCH
steps.prompt: Research [topic] for a [content type] targeting [audience]. Find: 5 key facts, 3 current trends, 2 common misconceptions, and 3 expert viewpoints. Format as bullet points.

steps.label: OUTLINE
steps.prompt: Using the research above, create a detailed outline for a [content type] targeting [audience]. Include: hook, 4-5 main sections with sub-points, and conclusion.

steps.label: DRAFT
steps.prompt: Write the full piece following this outline exactly. Tone: [your tone]. Length: [target]. Use specific data and examples from the research. No filler paragraphs.

steps.label: POLISH
steps.prompt: Review this draft as a senior editor. Score each section 1-10 on clarity, engagement, and accuracy. Fix everything below 8. Output the final version.

steps.label: ANALYZE
steps.prompt:
I'm considering [decision]. Analyze:
- 5 pros and 5 cons
- Financial implications (cost, revenue impact, ROI)
- Risk level for each factor (High/Medium/Low)
- What competitors have done in similar situations

steps.label: CHALLENGE
steps.prompt: Now act as a skeptical advisor. Poke holes in the analysis above. What am I missing? What could go wrong? What assumptions might be wrong? Rate each risk by likelihood and severity.

steps.label: DECIDE
steps.prompt:
Based on both the analysis and the critique, give me your final recommendation. Structure as:
- Decision: [Yes/No/Modify]
- Confidence level: [%]
- Top 3 conditions that must be true for success
- If I proceed: first 3 actions and timeline
- If I don't: best alternative

steps.label: AUDIENCE
steps.prompt: My target audience for this email campaign is [describe]. Their biggest pain point right now is [describe]. Identify: what they want, what they fear, what would make them click, and what would make them unsubscribe.

steps.label: SEQUENCE
steps.prompt: Based on this audience analysis, design a 5-email sequence. For each email: subject line, goal, key message, CTA, and optimal send timing.

steps.label: WRITE
steps.prompt: Now write emails #1 and #2 in full. Tone: [your tone]. Each under 200 words. Subject lines must create curiosity without being clickbait.

sections.pro_tip: Save your best chains as Claude Skills or Gemini Gems. Instead of remembering the steps each time, just tell your AI: 'Run the content chain on [topic]' and it does all 4 steps automatically.

sections.title: Advanced Skill 03: The Self-Improving Loop
sections.content: Make AI critique and improve its own work — automatically. AI's first draft is rarely its best work. But most people just accept whatever it generates. Power users know the secret: make AI be its own editor.

step_2.label: CRITIQUE
step_2.prompt:
Review what you just created. Act as a demanding client who is paying $5,000 for this work. Score it 1-10 on:
- Clarity: Is every point crystal clear?
- Specificity: Concrete examples or vague hand-waving?
- Engagement: Would the reader stay interested throughout?
- Accuracy: Any claims that aren't backed up?
- Structure: Does it flow logically?
- Originality: Does it say anything the reader hasn't heard before?

For each score below 8, explain EXACTLY what's wrong and provide a specific fix.

step_3.label: IMPROVE
step_3.prompt: Now rewrite it, applying every fix you identified. The new version must score 8+ on ALL criteria. Show me the updated scores alongside the final version.

more_templates.name: The Devil's Advocate
more_templates.prompt:
I'm planning to [decision/strategy/approach].

1. First, make the STRONGEST case FOR this plan
2. Then switch roles: make the STRONGEST case AGAINST it
3. For each criticism, rate severity (1-5) and suggest how to address it
4. Final verdict: Proceed / Modify / Abandon? Include your confidence level.

more_templates.name: The Customer Test
more_templates.prompt:
Review this [email/landing page/product description] as if you're my target customer: [describe them].

1. First impression in 3 seconds — would you keep reading?
2. What's compelling? What's confusing?
3. What questions do you still have after reading?
4. Would you [buy/click/sign up]? Be brutally honest.
5. Rewrite the weakest section to convert you.

more_templates.name: The Simplifier
more_templates.prompt:
Rewrite this [content] at a 6th-grade reading level without losing any of the key information. Then check:
- Did any important nuance get lost? If yes, add it back in simple terms.
- Is there any jargon remaining? Replace it.
- Read it aloud — does it flow naturally? Fix any awkward sentences.

sections.pro_tip: The Quality Loop works especially well as a Claude Skill. Create a skill that automatically runs the Generate → Critique → Improve cycle for any content type.

sections.title: Upsell / CTA
sections.content: You've just built your AI dream team. Continue your AI mastery journey.

items.description: 50+ ready-to-use prompts for ChatGPT, Claude & Gemini
Advanced multi-step workflows as installable skills
